# Development Station Quick Handoff (2026-05-25)

- Session intentionally stopped by user request for a fresh restart in a new chat.
- Active scope at stop: Rev-B HAT U9 migration stabilization only.
- Main source of truth: HANDOFF.md

What changed in this stop window:
- Updated Rev-B U9-adjacent label semantics:
  - TXD -> UART1_TX
  - RXD -> UART1_RX
  - GPIO8/BOOT -> BOOT0
  - GPIO9/BOOT -> BOOT1
- Added debug/program access support on HAT Rev-B schematic:
  - Labels: SWDIO, SWDCLK, NRST
  - Test points: TP_UART1_TX, TP_UART1_RX, TP_SWDIO, TP_SWDCLK, TP_NRST, TP_BOOT0
- Updated docs/STM32_BLUEPILL_PIN_TABLE.md validation log.
- File diagnostics are clean on edited files.

Important open risk:
- U9 symbol migration pin metadata and actual connected net ownership are not yet fully reconciled.
- Do not finalize BOOT0 default strap or reset RC behavior until pin-by-pin reconciliation is complete.

Known command status:
- Strict baseline connector check currently exits 1 (expected in strict mode for this repo state).

Next session first move:
1. Read HANDOFF.md and NEW_CHAT_HANDOFF.rmd.
2. Reconcile U9 pin-by-pin ownership in DSP-Regulator-HAT-RevB.kicad_sch.
3. Then finalize BOOT0 default-low and NRST deterministic network.

Paste prompt:
Continue Rev-B HAT U9 migration closure from HANDOFF.md and NEW_CHAT_HANDOFF.rmd. First perform explicit U9 pin-by-pin reconciliation in DSP-Regulator-HAT-RevB.kicad_sch, then implement final BOOT0 default strap and NRST reset network, then re-export netlist and run verification checks. Keep scope strictly on migration closure.
